title: cardamine caledonica
intro: Cardamine caledonica, also known as wood sorrel, is a perennial plant species belonging to the family Brassicaceae, characterized by its heart-shaped leaves and white or pinkish flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to treat urinary tract infections and kidney stones due to its high concentration of oxalic acid. The health benefits of Cardamine caledonica include its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation and improve overall well-being. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and salves, and can also be consumed as a fresh herb or dried for later use.
